threshold_rule_note: Model architecture/optimizer were selected on VALIDATION only (scripts/select_model.py); the test set was evaluated once. The entry-threshold RULE, however, was not: the frozen validation cutoff (timing_ensemble) turned out degenerate on test - the signal's level shifts (validation mean 0.00 vs test mean -0.79), so it never trades and returns exactly 0. The rolling-window rule (past-only, level-invariant) was adopted after observing that, so its +0.62 Sharpe carries selection optimism and should be read as an upper bound, not a clean out-of-sample estimate. Its 95% CI spans zero and it still loses to buy-and-hold. All three rules are published unmodified.
